model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["indexing","topk","summaries"],"rationale":"Increase model capacity after the previous optimizer/schedule change regressed validation loss, while restoring the simpler baseline optimization settings.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Move to a deeper convolutional stack with a global-average-pooling head, which is a cleaner fit for CIFAR-10 than the current flattened MLP head, and trim the step size for the deeper network.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 3","new":"DEPTH = 4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        layers: list[nn.Module] = []
        in_ch = INPUT_CHANNELS
        out_ch = BASE_CHANNELS
        for _ in range(DEPTH):
            layers.append(ConvBlock(in_ch, out_ch, USE_BATCHNORM, DROPOUT_RATE))
            in_ch = out_ch
            out_ch = min(out_ch * CHANNEL_MULT, 512)
        self.features = nn.Sequential(*layers)
        feat_size = IMAGE_SIZE // (2**DEPTH)
        feat_dim = in_ch * feat_size * feat_size
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(feat_dim, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return self.classifier(self.features(x))","new":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        layers: list[nn.Module] = []
        in_ch = INPUT_CHANNELS
        out_ch = BASE_CHANNELS
        for _ in range(DEPTH):
            layers.append(ConvBlock(in_ch, out_ch, USE_BATCHNORM, DROPOUT_RATE))
            in_ch = out_ch
            out_ch = min(out_ch * CHANNEL_MULT, 512)
        self.features = nn.Sequential(*layers)
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1)
        self.classifier = nn.Linear(in_ch, NUM_CLASSES)

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.features(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        x = torch.flatten(x, 1)
        return self.classifier(x)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Widen the now-stronger GAP-backed CNN and slightly reduce the Adam step size to keep the larger model stable under the fixed step budget.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"],"rationale":"Return to the previously best-performing 4-block width and step size, then add stronger Adam weight decay to regularize the GAP-backed model without reintroducing the broader schedule/optimizer changes that regressed performance.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Keep the current regularized GAP CNN, but use the fixed-step budget more effectively by turning on cosine decay and slightly raising the initial Adam step size to compensate for the decay.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"Nudge the scheduled Adam setup a bit more aggressive by raising the initial learning rate and slightly easing weight decay, which should help the current GAP CNN fit a little faster without changing the working architecture.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 4e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"Use larger minibatches under the fixed-step regime so each of the limited optimizer updates sees more CIFAR-10 examples and has lower gradient noise.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Scale the effective update batch further under the fixed-step budget and raise the Adam step size slightly so the larger-batch regime still makes meaningful progress each update.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"The current run is near the 1.0 barrier, so this is a small step-size and regularization retune aimed at extracting a bit more fit from the already-working large-batch cosine-annealed Adam setup.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 4e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Push the large-batch regime one step further and compensate with a slightly higher Adam step size, since the current setup is already near the 1.0 barrier and appears to benefit from more data per update.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"The model is already strong at the larger batch scale, so this is a small push toward faster fitting with slightly less regularization to see if the last bit of validation loss can be trimmed.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.0e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The current run is already near the best seen, so a small step-size increase is the simplest remaining knob to test for a marginal gain under the fixed-step cosine schedule.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.0e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Keep the cosine schedule shape but relax the final floor a bit so the last portion of the fixed-step run still has enough learning rate to make progress.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.05,","new":"        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.1,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Keep the cosine schedule but lower its final floor so the late-stage updates can settle more tightly instead of staying too hot until the end.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.1,","new":"        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.02,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"Restore the better cosine-floor setting and make a small upward learning-rate nudge, since the larger-batch run has been most responsive to step-size changes.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.3e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.02,","new":"        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.05,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"Continue the upward step-size search under the same large-batch cosine schedule, since the recent improvements have come from slightly larger Adam updates.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.3e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}
